Name: Iron
Comments: Iron supplements are primarily used to treat and prevent iron-deficiency anemia by increasing iron levels in the blood, which boosts red blood cell production, oxygen transport, and hemoglobin levels. They help alleviate symptoms like fatigue, dizziness, weakness, and shortness of breath, while also improving energy, muscle endurance, and cognitive function.

Name: Arginine AKG
Comments: Arginine Alpha-Ketoglutarate (AAKG) is a popular sports supplement combining L-arginine and alpha-ketoglutarate to boost nitric oxide, enhancing blood flow, nutrient delivery, and "muscle pumps" during workouts. It is primarily used to improve exercise performance, increase strength, and accelerate recovery.

Name: Urolithin A
Comments: Urolithin A is a gut metabolite, derived from ellagitannins found in foods like pomegranates, that enhances mitochondrial health by promoting mitophagy (the clearing of old mitochondria).  Clinical studies show it improves muscle strength (~10-12%), increases exercise endurance, reduces inflammation, and improves immune function, particularly in older adults.

Name: Resveratrol
Comments: Resveratrol is a natural plant polyphenol found in grapes, berries, and peanuts, widely known for its potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging properties. It is primarily used to support heart health by improving blood pressure and cholesterol, protect against neurodegenerative diseases, manage blood sugar, and potentially improve skin health.

Name: Omega 3
Comments: Omega-3 fatty acids are essential fats that significantly, improve heart health by lowering triglycerides, reducing blood pressure, and preventing plaque buildup. They are crucial for brain function, fighting inflammation, and supporting eye health. Key sources include fatty fish (salmon, mackerel) and seeds/nuts.

Name: Creatine
Comments: Creatine is often used by athletes involved in high-intensity intermittent activities that require a rapid recovery during training and competition. Injury prevention. Oral creatine might reduce the frequency of dehydration, muscle cramping, and injuries to the muscles, bones, ligaments, tendons and nerves.

Name: Collagen Bovine
Comments: Collagen supplements, often in hydrolyzed peptide form, are popular for reducing skin wrinkles, increasing hydration, and improving elasticity. They also support joint health by potentially reducing pain, increasing bone density, strengthening brittle nails and hair, and potentially improving gut health.

Name: Papaya Enzime
Comments: Papaya enzymes, primarily papain, offer significant health benefits by breaking down proteins, aiding digestion, reducing inflammation, and promoting skin health. Commonly used in supplements, they relieve bloating, gas, and indigestion. Topically, they act as a gentle, natural exfoliant that removes dead skin cells, unclogs pores, and brightens skin
